qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary Java class loading via java_import in JdbcDriver allows code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets lib/arjdbc/jdbc/driver.rb java_import(driver_class_name) class loading, not the advisory-described lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb sql.gsub() SQL sanitization flaw.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unvalidated JNDI lookup in configure_jndi allows remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets lib/arjdbc/jdbc/connection.rb InitialContext.lookup(config[:jndi]) JNDI lookup behavior, not the advisory-described lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb sql.gsub() SQL injection root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in PostgreSQL rename_table via unquoted table name interpolation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Although this is a SQL injection in the same package, it identifies lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb rename_table identifier interpolation, not the advisory-described lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb sql.gsub() bind-substitution sanitization flaw.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory concerns SQL injection caused by sql.gsub() bind substitution in lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb, while the final qualifying findings concern driver class loading, JNDI lookup, and a separate PostgreSQL rename_table interpolation issue.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5qw5-wf2q-f538 states the vulnerable component is lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb and the root cause is sql.gsub() not properly sanitizing user-supplied input before SQL queries.
Local source contains the advisory-relevant sql.gsub('?') bind substitution paths in lib/arjdbc/jdbc/adapter.rb:380, :391, and :416.

sql_injection_rename_table/vulnerability_report.json identifies lib/arjdbc/postgresql/adapter.rb:730 execute "ALTER TABLE #{name} RENAME TO #{new_name}", a different function and source-to-sink condition.
arbitrary_java_class_loading and jndi_injection identify non-SQL-injection code execution paths in driver.rb and connection.rb.
